description_long: This sculpture depicts the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head and a human body, seated cross-legged on a decorated pedestal. Ganesha is shown playing a stringed musical instrument, likely a veena, with both hands, and his trunk curves gracefully to one side. The figure is adorned with detailed jewelry, armlets, and a headdress, all intricately carved into the surface. The back of the sculpture reveals the continuation of the headdress and garment folds, with the pedestal featuring ornate patterns. The material appears to be a dark, weathered stone, giving the sculpture a sense of age and gravitas. The overall craftsmanship suggests a traditional style, with attention to anatomical and ornamental details.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha seated cross-legged, playing a stringed instrument, adorned with jewelry and a headdress.
description_view_0: Front view showing Ganesha's face, trunk, and both hands playing the instrument, with full visibility of the pedestal and decorative details.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of Ganesha's head, trunk, and the side of the instrument, with arm and leg contours visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of Ganesha's headdress, garment folds, and the ornate pedestal, with no facial features visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile of Ganesha's head and trunk, with the instrument and arm details from a different angle.
attribute_subject_matter: Ganesha, the Hindu elephant-headed deity, playing a musical instrument
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is isolated and does not provide environmental clues.
